div.h2: x86_64 Syscall Table — Number → Name → Argument Signature

g.text: INT = plain integer (read from register directly)
PTR* = struct pointer (deep decode or print as hex)
Nr | Name | nargs | arg1 | arg2 | arg3 | arg4 | arg5 | arg6
0 | read | 3 | FD fd | PTR* buf | INT count | | |
1 | write | 3 | FD fd | STR* buf | INT count | | |
2 | open | 3 | STR* path | FLG flags | INT mode | | |
7 | poll | 3 | PTR* fds | INT nfds | INT timeout | | |
8 | lseek | 3 | FD fd | INT offset | INT whence | | |
9 | mmap | 6 | PTR* addr | INT length | FLG prot | FLG flags | FD fd | INT offset
59 | execve | 3 | STR* path | PTR* argv | PTR* envp | | |
257 | openat | 4 | FD dirfd | STR* path | FLG flags | INT mode | |
Total entries: ~335 (sparse; gaps are reserved numbers)
4 — char* in tracee memory → PTRACE_PEEKDATA loop
6 — decode O_RDONLY or O_WRONLY or O_CREAT or...
7 — decode PROT_READ or PROT_WRITE or PROT_EXEC
8 — decode MAP_SHARED or MAP_PRIVATE or MAP_ANON
Alt: /proc/pid/mem pread() | 1 syscall for N bytes
ret | rax | INT | bytes read, or -errno
1 | rdi | STR* | PTRACE_PEEKDATA loop → quoted string
2 | rsi | FLG | O_RDONLY or O_WRONLY or O_CREAT or ...
ret | rax | FD or -err | fd >= 0 success, else -errno
3 | rdx | FLG(prot) | PROT_READ or PROT_WRITE or PROT_EXEC
4 | r10 (!) | FLG(flags) | MAP_SHARED or MAP_PRIVATE or MAP_ANON
ret | rax | PTR* or -err | mapped addr, or MAP_FAILED=-1
r10 not rcx — syscall ABI differs from C ABI for arg4
